title: Statistics of the Zeros of the Zeta Function: Mesoscopic and Macroscopic Phenomena
speaker: Brad Rodgers
abstract: We review the well known microscopic correspondence between random zeros of the Riemann zeta-function and the eigenvalues of random matrices, and discuss evidence that this correspondence extends to larger mesoscopic collections of zeros or eigenvalues. In addition, we discuss interesting phenomena that appears in the statistics of even larger macroscopic collections of zeros. The terms microscopic, mesoscopic, and macroscopic are from random matrix theory and will be defined in the talk.

proof_stages.label: Microscopic zero statistics and the GUE benchmark
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up the nontrivial zeros under RH, the Riemann-von Mangoldt density and microscopic scaling, Montgomery pair correlation, k-level/GUE density, and the unitary-matrix comparison together with known compact-Fourier-support confirmations.

proof_stages.label: Mesoscopic rigidity and the oscillation barrier
proof_stages.summary: The speaker formulates the guiding philosophy that overly oscillatory microscopic test functions are the hard obstruction, while longer-scale questions with band limitation should be accessible, illustrating this by Fujii’s mesoscopic CLT and the Costin-Lebowitz unitary-eigenvalue analogue.

proof_stages.label: Macroscopic variance saturation and low-zero troughs
proof_stages.summary: Using Backlund’s decomposition of the counting function through S(t), the talk explains the phase change to variance saturation and exhibits unscaled pair-difference histograms whose troughs occur at the ordinates of low-lying zeros.

proof_stages.label: Band-limited macroscopic pair-correlation refinement
proof_stages.summary: Montgomery’s statistic is reformulated to detect zeros separated by a fixed macroscopic distance but blurred at microscopic resolution, and a refined band-limited asymptotic is described whose zeta-prime-over-zeta and arithmetic terms produce the Bogomolny-Keating trough pattern.

proof_stages.label: Strong-Szego-type linear-statistic CLT for zeta zeros
proof_stages.summary: The same circle of ideas gives an analogue of the strong Szego theorem for general linear statistics of stretched zeta zeros, with the expected mean, a form-factor variance, and Gaussian limiting distribution under RH.

proof_stages.label: Explicit-formula and large-sieve proof mechanism
proof_stages.summary: The proof replaces raw zero counts by the oscillatory measure dS, applies an exact functional-equation-sensitive explicit formula relating it to prime logarithms, then uses Fourier smoothing and a Montgomery/large-sieve argument so prime/integer rigidity collapses the main double integral to the expected arithmetic sum.

proof_stages.label: Moments and possible extensions
proof_stages.summary: As an application and open direction, the speaker asks whether macroscopic band-limited k-point correlation information can recover the Keating-Snaith moment conjectures beyond the known k=1,2 cases, and then comments in Q&A on possible extensions to other L-functions.
